headline: Intel Tumbles 7% Despite BoA Double Upgrade, AMD Slides 6% as AI Infrastructure Deal Can’t Stop Profit-Taking
summary: Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) are down 7% at midday on Tuesday, while Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) stock is sliding 6%. The pullback comes after a torrid multi-session rally in both names. Intel stock closed yesterday at $127.86, having climbed 18% over the past month. AMD stock ended Monday at $547.26, an all-time high. No fresh ... Intel Tumbles 7% Despite BoA Double Upgrade, AMD Slides 6% as AI Infrastructure Deal Can’t Stop Profit-Taking

headline: Global Investors Are Looking Past Rate-Hike Bets and Still Betting on the AI Boom
summary: The world’s biggest investors are still buying stocks, even as the U.S.-Iran war keeps inflation risks high.

headline: Kansas City Current, BofA Announces Multi-Year Partnership Renewal Featuring Enhanced Community Efforts
summary: The Kansas City Current and Bank of America announced a multi-year partnership renewal on Tuesday, set to celebrate community impact and grow the game of soccer nationwide. Bank of America will continue being an official partner of the club as part of the announcement, and the Current will support Bank of America's landmark global sports program, Sports with Us.

headline: No “big top” yet in risk assets, BofA says despite elevated sentiment
summary: Investing.com -- Investor sentiment has surged to near-peak levels but has not yet reached the threshold that historically signals a major market top, according to Bank of America's latest Global Fund Manager Survey.

headline: Williams Sonoma (WSM) Stock Could Be 8% Overvalued After Bank Of America Buy Call
summary: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) stock is back in focus after Bank of America reinitiated coverage with a positive view on the retailer’s role as an affordable luxury brand that serves a relatively steady customer base. See our latest analysis for Williams-Sonoma. headline: Goldman-Backed Go Rises 10% After Biggest Japan IPO This Year

headline: China’s Property Stocks Tumble Back to Pre-2024 Stimulus Levels

headline: Bank Of America Targets Elder Fraud With AI And Nationwide Scaminars
summary: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) is rolling out a nationwide "Scaminars" program, with 1,000 in-person events scheduled across the US through 2026. The seminars focus on helping older adults recognize and avoid scams and financial fraud. The bank is using more than 50 AI-powered fraud detection systems alongside large scale consumer education to support the effort. For a large retail bank like Bank of America, elder financial fraud is both a client risk and a reputational issue. This "Scaminars"...
